<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alloy Orb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2963)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a metal weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Although solid, this orb of metal swirls with bright silver and dark iron colors, as if made of liquid. When you activate the _alloy orb_, select [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277). The affixed weapon functions as the chosen material for 1 minute, suppressing its original material. Powerful weapons overwhelm the magic of this talisman, and it works only on weapons of 8th level or lower.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Alloy Orb (Low-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Alloy Orb (Standard-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This orb works on weapons of 15th level or lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Exquisite Alloy Orb (Standard-Grade)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate this orb, you can select adamantine, cold iron, or dawnsilver. This orb works on weapons of 15th level or lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Alloy Orb (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

**Price** 1,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This orb works on weapons of any level.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Exquisite Alloy Orb (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

---

<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Although solid, this orb of metal swirls with bright silver and dark iron colors, as if made of liquid. When you activate the _alloy orb_, select [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277). The affixed weapon functions as the chosen material for 1 minute, suppressing its original material. Powerful weapons overwhelm the magic of this talisman, and it works only on weapons of 8th level or lower.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Basilisk Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2964)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) a melee attack with the affixed shield.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This slimy green stone glows with whenever the shield it adorns blocks a melee attack. When the eye is activated, the attacker must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 for 1 minute as its body slowly stiffens in partial petrification.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Basilisk Eye

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bloodseeker Beak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2965)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 263

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You hit a [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creature with the affixed weapon

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, hollow proboscis is harvested from the notorious bloodseeker beast and drips a trickle of blood. When you activate the beak, you deal an extra 1d4 precision damage on your damage roll. If you deal sneak attack damage to the creature, you also deal 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pendant is forged from grainy steel and depicts a snorting bull's face. The pendant must be attached to the chest area or on a shoulder guard. When you activate the pendant, attempt an Athletics check to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) with a +1 item bonus to check. Increase the distance you Shove your target to 10 feet on a success or 20 feet on a critical success.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bronze Bull Pendant

When you activate this alabaster pendant, attempt to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) using Medicine with a +1 item bonus to the check. If you succeed, and you were trying to stabilize, the target regains 1 Hit Point, losing the dying condition and becoming conscious as normal. If you succeed, and you were trying to stop bleeding, the bleeding ends.

This knot of copper wire reshapes itself in a new pattern every time its affixed weapon deals damage. When you activate the coil, the damaged creature must succeed at a DC 31 Will save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) 1. If it critically fails, it instead becomes stunned 2.

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 264 **Price** 2,400 gp **Usage** affixed to a weapon **Activate**([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Trigger** Your Strike damages a target --- Made from a treated sliver of cold iron, this talisman allows you to counteract magical effects. When you activate the dispelling sliver, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a single spell active on the target (counteract modifier +29), with the effects of an 8th-rank [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell.
If you activate the talisman on a successful [Dispelling Slice](/Feats.aspx?ID=594), the talisman attempts to counteract all spells active on the target, in addition to your attempt from Dispelling Slice.
name: Dispelling Sliver

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Effervescent Ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2971)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 264

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You're trained in Acrobatics
</row>

</column>

---

Light spring water fizzes and bubbles within this small glass globe, spilling onto the affixed armor when activated. Until the end of your turn, you can move across water and other liquids as if they were solid ground. Your movement does not trigger any device or hazard that relies on a weight-sensitive pressure plate or similar device. When the ampoule's effect ends, you sink, fall, break through flimsy ground, or land on pressure plates as normal for your current location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Effervescent Ampoule

This metal grasshopper studded with emeralds is usually clasped to the legs of a suit of armor. When you activate it, make a [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), traveling up to 40 feet horizontally and up to 10 feet vertically. If you don't end your jump on solid ground, you flutter in the air until the end of your turn, then fall harmlessly at a rate of 60 feet per round until you reach the ground.

This metal grasshopper studded with emeralds is usually clasped to the legs of a suit of armor. When you activate it, make a [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), traveling up to 40 feet horizontally and up to 10 feet vertically. markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 265 **Price** 50 gp **Usage** affixed to a weapon **Activate**([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) --- This cube of blackened iron is affixed to a weapon with an iron chain. When you activate the cube, make a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, you can attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) the creature you hit. If this knocks the target prone, the target takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage from the force of the impact. If you're wielding a two-handed melee weapon, you can ignore Trip's requirement that you have a free hand. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you've made both of them.
If you have the [Knockdown](/Feats.aspx?ID=372) feat, ignore the normal size restrictions for the Trip and increase the damage dice if you knock the target [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) to the damage die of your weapon if it's higher than d6.
name: Iron Cube

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 265

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</row>

---

This small medallion is shaped like a shield. When you activate it, you gain a +2 status bonus to the triggering save and other saves against fear for 1 minute. On the triggering save, if the outcome of your roll is a failure, you get a success instead. If the outcome is a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

</column>

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 266 **Price** 175 gp **Usage** affixed to a weapon **Activate**([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) --- This talisman is the magically treated corpse of a tiny bat bound in papyrus. When activated, the affixed weapon detects vibrations around you and guides your perception. For 1 minute, you don't need to succeed at a flat check to target [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) creatures. You're not [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to creatures that are hidden from you (unless you're off-guard to them for reasons other than the hidden condition), and you need only a successful DC 5 flat check to target a [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) creature. While you're adjacent to an undetected creature of your level or lower, it's instead only hidden from you. If you have the [Blind-Fight](/Feats.aspx?ID=388) feat, you gain imprecise
echolocation with a range of 30 feet for 1 minute. This makes creatures that would be undetected by you because you can't see them hidden instead.
name: Mummified Bat

This black strand of leather is tied to look like a peace knot when the weapon is worn, but it doesn't hamper drawing the weapon. When you activate the knot, the creature you damaged takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the bleed ends. If you have the [Twist the Knife](/Feats.aspx?ID=570) feat, the talisman instead deals persistent bleed damage equal to your sneak attack damage.

</column>

This dried-seaweed bracelet is lined with charms shaped like small shark teeth. When you activate the bracelet, attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) using Acrobatics with a +1 item bonus to the check. If you roll a success, you get a critical success instead (if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead). If you fail the Acrobatics check against a grabbing creature, the creature must either release you as a free action or take 2d8 piercing damage as shark's teeth momentarily emerge from your skin.

---

When you activate this resin-strengthened viper skull, make a melee Strike against the triggering creature. If you have Reactive Strike, you can activate the viper's fang as a free action. If your attack is a critical hit and the trigger was a manipulate action, you disrupt that action. This Strike doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike. If you hit with this attack, the creature is exposed to viper fang venom.

 **Viper Fang Venom** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)); **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 32; **Maximum Duration** 4 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d10 poison damage and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) 1.

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 267

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You successfully Trip a foe

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wolf canine is bound in a strip of leather and tied to a buckle or strap on a suit of armor. When you activate the fang, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier to the target of your Trip.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wolf Fang

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 75 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- This tapered candle has a golden wick that burns with white fire. You activate the candle by lighting it, which causes creatures within 10 feet of the candle to find it difficult to tell falsehoods. Creatures in the area receive a –4 status penalty to Lie.
In addition, when first entering the affected area, each creature (including you) must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be unable to tell any deliberate lies while within 10 feet of the lit candle. This lasts for as long as the candle is lit. Once lit, the candle burns for 10 minutes, and it cannot be extinguished.
name: Candle of Truth

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- This blueberry-sized bead appears to be a sphere of glass with a flickering light at its core. In truth, the “glass” is a bubble of solidified time magic, containing suspended lava at the exact point before a volcanic eruption. When activated, it becomes a tiny beacon of bright light before unleashing its power. After you Activate _frozen lava_, it quickly heats up. If you or anyone else hurls it (an Interact action), it detonates as a fireball where it lands. Your toss can place the center of the fireball anywhere within 70 feet, though at the GM's discretion, you might need to make an attack roll if the throw is unusually challenging. If no one hurls the bead by the start of your next turn, it pops like an ostentatious but harmless firework.
_Frozen lava_ comes in many varieties, made of lava taken from notable volcanoes on Golarion and across the planes. Frozen Lava **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 30 gp --- 5d6 damage (DC 21) Frozen Lava of Blackpeak **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 70 gp --- 7d6 damage (DC 25) Frozen Lava of Pale Mountain **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 150 gp --- 9d6 damage (DC 27) Frozen Lava of Mhar Massif **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 300 gp --- 11d6 damage (DC 30) Frozen Lava of Droskar's Crag **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 600 gp --- 13d6 damage (DC 32) Frozen Lava of Ka **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 1,300 gp --- 15d6 damage (DC 36) Frozen Lava of Sakalayo **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 3,000 gp --- 17d6 damage (DC 39) Frozen Lava of Barrowsiege **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 8,000 gp --- 19d6 damage (DC 43)
name: Frozen Lava

Your toss can place the center of the fireball anywhere within 70 feet, though at the GM's discretion, you might need to make an attack roll if the throw is unusually challenging. If no one hurls the bead by the start of your next turn, it pops like an ostentatious but harmless firework.
_Frozen lava_ comes in many varieties, made of lava taken from notable volcanoes on Golarion and across the planes. Frozen Lava **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 30 gp --- 5d6 damage (DC 21) Frozen Lava of Blackpeak **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 70 gp --- 7d6 damage (DC 25) Frozen Lava of Pale Mountain **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 150 gp --- 9d6 damage (DC 27) Frozen Lava of Mhar Massif **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 300 gp --- 11d6 damage (DC 30) Frozen Lava of Droskar's Crag **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 600 gp --- 13d6 damage (DC 32) Frozen Lava of Ka **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 1,300 gp --- 15d6 damage (DC 36) Frozen Lava of Sakalayo **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 3,000 gp --- 17d6 damage (DC 39) Frozen Lava of Barrowsiege **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268 **Price** 8,000 gp --- 19d6 damage (DC 43)
name: Frozen Lava of Barrowsiege

---

This vial contains water blessed by a benevolent deity. You activate a vial of _holy water_ by throwing it as a Strike. It's a simple thrown weapon with a range increment of 20 feet. _Holy water_ deals 1d6 spirit damage and 1 spirit splash damage. _Holy water_ can damage only creatures with the unholy trait.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Marvelous Miniature](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3002)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Every _marvelous miniature_ is an exceptionally small replica of a real creature or object. The miniature is made from wood, pewter, or other simple materials, and features a rune etched into the underside of the replica's base. _Marvelous miniatures_ sometimes come packaged together; for example, the camping set features the boat, campfire, and horse miniatures. Activating a _marvelous miniatur_e causes it to transform into another creature or object, which then can be used as normal for that object. Each miniature can be activated only once, with most of them permanently becoming the item in their description.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Boat)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 76 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This miniature can be activated only when tossed into a large body of water, such as a lake or broad river. It transforms into a [cutter](/Vehicles.aspx?ID=41). The cutter lasts 1 day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Campfire)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms into an active campfire. The campfire doesn't require attending and remains fully lit for 8 hours, though it can be quenched as any other campfire.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Chest)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you use this miniature, a small wooden chest appears and immediately opens. This chest can hold up to 10 Bulk worth of items. Once the chest is closed, it transforms again—this time into a key—taking all of the stored items with it. You can activate this key by spending an Interact action to turn it in an imaginary lock, which causes it to transform back into a chest with all of the items still inside. Once it has turned into a chest a second time, it forever remains a wooden chest.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Horse)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms into a horse. The horse can't attack or use reactions, but otherwise uses all the statistics of a [riding horse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=266) and follows your basic commands. The horse doesn't need to eat or drink. After 8 hours, it reverts back to miniature form, then crumbles to dust.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Ladder)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms permanently into a 20-foot-long wooden ladder.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Swift Sparrow)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 210

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms into a seemingly mundane sparrow. The sparrow flies to a destination of your choice within 200 feet that you can see, then [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) in a 30-foot burst using your Perception modifier before returning. The sparrow then unravels into a small map of the area it perceived, including any creatures it saw. The map is rudimentary and doesn't reveal anything the sparrow failed to perceive.
name: Marvelous Miniature

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Marvelous Miniature](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3002)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Every _marvelous miniature_ is an exceptionally small replica of a real creature or object. The miniature is made from wood, pewter, or other simple materials, and features a rune etched into the underside of the replica's base. _Marvelous miniatures_ sometimes come packaged together; for example, the camping set features the boat, campfire, and horse miniatures. Activating a _marvelous miniatur_e causes it to transform into another creature or object, which then can be used as normal for that object. Each miniature can be activated only once, with most of them permanently becoming the item in their description.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Boat)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 76 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This miniature can be activated only when tossed into a large body of water, such as a lake or broad river. It transforms into a [cutter](/Vehicles.aspx?ID=41). The cutter lasts 1 day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Campfire)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms into an active campfire. The campfire doesn't require attending and remains fully lit for 8 hours, though it can be quenched as any other campfire.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Chest)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you use this miniature, a small wooden chest appears and immediately opens. This chest can hold up to 10 Bulk worth of items. Once the chest is closed, it transforms again—this time into a key—taking all of the stored items with it. You can activate this key by spending an Interact action to turn it in an imaginary lock, which causes it to transform back into a chest with all of the items still inside. Once it has turned into a chest a second time, it forever remains a wooden chest.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Horse)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms into a horse. The horse can't attack or use reactions, but otherwise uses all the statistics of a [riding horse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=266) and follows your basic commands. The horse doesn't need to eat or drink. After 8 hours, it reverts back to miniature form, then crumbles to dust.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Ladder)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 268

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms permanently into a 20-foot-long wooden ladder.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Marvelous Miniature (Swift Sparrow)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 210

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this miniature transforms into a seemingly mundane sparrow. The sparrow flies to a destination of your choice within 200 feet that you can see, then [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) in a 30-foot burst using your Perception modifier before returning. The sparrow then unravels into a small map of the area it perceived, including any creatures it saw. The map is rudimentary and doesn't reveal anything the sparrow failed to perceive.
name: Marvelous Miniature (Horse)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flat piece of hard stone is specially prepared for etching a magical fundamental rune or property rune. You can etch only one rune upon a stone. When a rune is transferred from the runestone to another object, the runestone cracks and is destroyed. The Price listed is for an empty stone; a stone holding a rune adds the Price of the rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spirit Bulb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3004)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 269

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical bulb is harvested from an ancient grove rich in primal plant magic. When you activate the bulb, you either eat it to have it cast a 5th-rank [_plant form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=223) spell affecting you, or plant it in the ground next to you to have it cast a 5th-rank [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325) spell. If you choose the summoning option, the plant or fungus appears where you planted the bulb, and you can Sustain the activation to keep control of the creature.

When you activate the bulb, you either eat it to have it cast a 5th-rank [_plant form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=223) spell affecting you, or plant it in the ground next to you to have it cast a 5th-rank [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325) spell. If you choose the summoning option, the plant or fungus appears where you planted the bulb, and you can Sustain the activation to keep control of the creature.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=586" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 269

**Price** 110 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

---

This item looks like a normal trident carved with Gozren motifs. If thrown without being activated, it wobbles in the air and fails to strike true. When you Activate the trident, the carvings crackle with electricity. You then hurl the trident. It shatters immediately after leaving your hand and unleashes its magic as a 4th-rank [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172) originating from your space. The bolt deals 5d12 electricity damage and has a basic Reflex save DC of 25.

</column>

A malicious deity's malice lies within this vial of water. You activate a vial of _unholy water_ by throwing it as a Strike. It's a simple thrown weapon with a range increment of 20 feet. _Unholy_ water deals 1d6 spirit damage and 1 spirit splash damage. _Unholy water_ can damage only creatures with the holy trait.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=532" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

This thick leather belt is engraved with imagery of an ancient tree. You gain 15 temporary Hit Points the first time you invest the belt in a day. When you invest the belt, you either increase your Constitution modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. 

**Activate—Call Upon the Ancient Life** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw upon the life- giving energy of the tree on the belt to gain rapid healing. For 2d4 rounds, at the start of your turn each round, you recover 15 Hit Points.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Belt of Long Life

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bracers of Strength](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3008)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=532" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">

---

Etchings of powerful bears decorate these brass bracers. You gain a +3 item bonus to Athletics checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to lift a heavy object, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), and [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34). When you invest the bracers, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. 

**Activate—Bear Hug** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** Attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a creature. If you succeed, you crush the creature in your grasp, dealing bludgeoning damage to it equal to your Strength modifier. If you critically succeeded, the damage is equal to double your Strength modifier and the creature [suffocates](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439) as long as it remains grabbed or restrained by you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bracers of Strength

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=532" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

This thin cloak is surprisingly light, as if clouds or the very wind were woven together to make the garment. The cloak grants you a +3 item bonus to Acrobatics checks. When you invest the cloak, you either increase your Dexterity modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate—Ride the Wind** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tug on the cloak, wrapping yourself in the power of wind. You gain a fly Speed of 30 feet for 1 hour. While wrapped in the cloak, you become translucent, causing you to become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) for the duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cloak of Swiftness

---

A trio of brilliant gems have been set into this elegant golden crown. You gain a +3 item bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge, regardless of the skill. When you invest the crown, you either increase your Intelligence modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. This gives you additional trained skills and languages, as normal for increasing your Intelligence modifier. You must select skills and languages the first time you invest the crown, and whenever you invest the same crown of intellect, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time. 

**Activate—Search Your Mind** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain the effects of [_hypercognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=156).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This simple scarf is designed for wrapping around the head and comes with a gemmed pin for decoration. When you invest the headwrap, you either increase your Wisdom modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. 

**Activate—Consider the Consequences** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** When you are considering a course of action, you get a gut feeling about whether it's a good idea. You gain the effects of an [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) spell, except that you receive the result from your own instincts rather than an external source. 

**Activate—Reclaim Your Mind** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail a saving throw against an effect that makes you [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) ;  **Effect** The _headwrap of wisdom_ clears your mind. You can reroll the saving throw and use the better result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Headwrap of Wisdom

Several sapphires are set along the length of this brilliant silver necklace. The necklace features an intricately carved pendant in the shape of a wolf's head. You gain a +2 item bonus to Deception and Diplomacy checks. When you invest the necklace, you either increase your Charisma modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. 

**Activate—Win Them Over** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 4th-rank [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) spell (DC 38).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Necklace of Allure

When you affix these simple iron horseshoes to the hooves of an ordinary horse or a quadrupedal animal companion and the animal companion invests them, that creature gains a +5-foot item bonus to its land Speed and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37). In addition, when it [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), it can move 5 feet farther if jumping horizontally or 3 feet higher if jumping vertically.

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 272 **Price** 900 gp **Usage** worn barding **Bulk** L --- This light barding is covered in stylized wind motifs. When you suit up your animal companion, the barding adjusts to fit your animal companion regardless of its shape.
When your companion falls, wind picks it up from below; it gains the effects of _gentle landing_. **Activate—Take Flight** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You trace a finger along the wind motifs on the barding, granting your companion wearing the barding a fly Speed of 30 feet for 10 minutes. Even if the companion doesn't have the mount special ability, it can still Fly while being ridden.
name: Barding of the Zephyr

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Collar of Empathy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3015)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Companion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=558" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 272

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn collar (companion) and worn bracelet (you)

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate collar of intertwined leather strips of contrasting colors is paired with a bracelet of a similar construction. When you wear and invest the bracelet and your companion wears and invests the collar, you gain a stronger connection to each other. You and your companion can always sense each other's emotional states and basic physical wants and needs. 

**Activate—Empathic Link** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** You perceive through your animal companion's senses instead of your own. You can Sustain the activation. You are unaware of your own surroundings for as long as you are using your animal companion's senses. In addition to the obvious use when you are separated from your companion, this ability might allow you to notice sounds, scents, and other stimuli that your companion's senses register but yours alone don't.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Collar of Empathy

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Collar of Inconspicuousness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3016)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Companion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=558" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 272

**Price** 475 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn collar

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This leather collar's almost threadbare look belies its magical nature. When your companion wears and invests the collar, it gains the ability to change its appearance from that of a ferocious animal into a more inconspicuous form. 

**Activate—Adorable Guise** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** You touch your animal companion to transform it into a nonthreatening Tiny creature of the same family or a similar creature (for instance, a house cat instead of a tiger, or a puppy instead of a wolf). This has the effects of [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=217) (2nd rank, or 4th rank if your companion can fly). The effect lasts until you Dismiss it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Collar of Inconspicuousness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bottled Air](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3017)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273

**Price** 320 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Appearing to be an ordinary corked glass bottle, this item contains a limitless supply of fresh air. You must uncork the bottle with an Interact action before you can activate it. 

**Activate—Breath In** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You draw a breath of air from the bottle. This allows you to breathe even in an airless or toxic environment. Air doesn't escape the mouth of the bottle, so leaving the open bottle in an airless environment doesn't change the environment.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bottled Air

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

This small bag is filled with a fine, silvery powder that feels silky to the touch. 

**Activate—Disperse** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You scatter the dust into the air around you, causing it to condense into a cloud in a 20-foot burst within 10 feet, as the [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) spell. You can Sustain the activation to make the cloud Fly 20 feet. The cloud lasts 1 minute, and you can Dismiss it.

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- This polished crystal sphere enhances scrying magic. Any visual information received through a spell with the [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141) trait that was cast by the _crystal ball_ appears within the sphere, and any auditory information sounds out from the surface of the sphere. When you cast a spell with the scrying trait by any other means while holding the sphere, you can relay any information you receive in the same way, allowing others to see or hear the target.
The base version of a _crystal ball_ is a sphere of clear quartz, but other versions are made of different stones. **Activate—Clairvoyance** 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The crystal ball casts [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=40) to your specifications. **Activate—Scrying** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** twice per day; **Effect** The crystal ball casts a DC 33 [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=268) spell to your specifications. Crystal Ball (Clear Quartz) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 572 **Price** 3,800 gp Crystal Ball (Selenite) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 572 **Price** 7,000 gp --- Scrying is DC 36 and gives you the benefits of [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) on the target. Crystal Ball (Moonstone) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 572 **Price** 7,500 gp --- Scrying is DC 37 and gives you the benefits of [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) on the target, using the same save DC. Crystal Ball (Peridot) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 572 **Price** 12,500 gp --- Scrying is DC 39 and gives you the benefits of [_telepathy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719) for communicating with the target. Crystal Ball (Obsidian) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 572 **Price** 32,000 gp --- Scrying is DC 41 and gives you the benefits of [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) for anything you watch through it.
name: Crystal Ball

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eternal Eruption](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3020)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Resembling [frozen lava](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3000), an eternal eruption is made with the same type of time magic, but is built to loop through time, reforming itself after it's used. Determining the difference between the two requires a close examination to see a faint, repeating pattern of red runes.  

**Activate—Lava Bomb** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You fling the eternal eruption, with the effect of frozen lava of the same item level. After 2d4 hours, the eternal eruption reforms itself in a container on your person, typically the one you most recently stored it in.

---

Resembling [frozen lava](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3000), an eternal eruption is made with the same type of time magic, but is built to loop through time, reforming itself after it's used. Determining the difference between the two requires a close examination to see a faint, repeating pattern of red runes.  

**Activate—Lava Bomb** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You fling the eternal eruption, with the effect of frozen lava of the same item level. After 2d4 hours, the eternal eruption reforms itself in a container on your person, typically the one you most recently stored it in.

    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Everlight Crystal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3021)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _everlight crystal_ is one of the most common applications of permanent magic. This stone or gem sheds magical [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) constantly in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet). The light requires no oxygen, generates no heat, and can’t be extinguished, though the crystal can be covered.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Everlight Crystal

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 273 **Price** 880 gp **Bulk** 1 (when not activated) --- Before activation, this item appears to be nothing more than a simple rolled-up tent, barely large enough to fit four Medium creatures. Despite attempts to clean it, the tent is perpetually smudged with dirt in various places. **Activate—Unroll** 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The rolled-up tent expands into a spacious yurt complete with a fire pit, 10 bedrolls, various cooking utensils, and basic food and water.
The yurt can house and feed you and up to nine other Medium creatures that eat roughly as much as a human does; they need not attempt a Survival check to Subsist when you use the yurt. Fires and light inside the yurt do not extend illumination into the area surrounding the yurt, making it harder to spot from a distance.
name: Explorer's Yurt

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 274 **Price** 1,900 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This broom has a tenuous connection to gravity, and it tends to drift even while stowed. You can ride on the broom using one hand to guide it, and the broom can carry up to one passenger in addition to you. The broom moves at a fly Speed of 20 feet. The broom can carry only so much, taking a –10-foot penalty to its Speed if laden with more than 20 Bulk, and crashing to the ground if it carries more than 30 Bulk. **Activate—Lift Off** (concentrate, manipulate) ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** You name a destination on the same plane, and the broom speeds toward it at a fly Speed of 40 feet. You must either clutch the broom with two hands in order to ride it, or you need to release the broom to send it off with no rider. If you don't have a good idea of the location, layout, and general direction of the destination, or if your named destination is on another plane, the broom wanders aimlessly, circling back to its starting location after 30 minutes.
If the broom carries a rider, this activation lasts until 4 hours pass (typically 16 miles of travel), the broom reaches its destination, or you Dismiss the activation. If the broom doesn't have a rider, the activation lasts until the broom reaches its destination. When the activation ends, the broom floats to the ground and can't be activated again for 1 hour.
name: Flying Broomstick

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Horn of Exorcism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3024)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 274

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _horn of exorcism_ is an instrument made from an animal horn, or an object of the same shape from carved wood or ivory. 

**Activate—Rattle the Dead** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Blowing into the horn frightens ghosts and evil spirits who can hear its call. Make an Intimidation check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) against all creatures with the undead or unholy trait in a 30-foot emanation. This can affect even a mindless creature with that trait, and you don't take a penalty when you attempt to Demoralize a creature that doesn't understand your language. 

**Activate—Sacred Seeds** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You fill the horn with sacred seeds and then scatter them around you with a twist of your wrist. The horn grants you and your allies in a 30-foot emanation the [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune on all of your weapon and unarmed Strikes for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Horn of Exorcism

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 274 **Price** 459 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- This top has 20 divisions, painted in a chaotic mess of clashing colors. When spun, the top quickly settles on a side and generates a strange magical effect based on the side that lands face-up. **Activate—Spin the Top** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** Choose a creature within 60 feet to target and roll a d20 on the table below to determine the top's effect. You make any decisions for a spell cast by the top unless otherwise indicated, except that it must target the creature you chose, or the creature you chose must be the center of the spell's area, if it has an area but no targets. If the spell's range is less than 60 feet, increase the range to 60 feet.
Any spell DC required is DC 27, and any spell attack roll required is +17. If the top casts a spell on you, you don't get a saving throw or other defense against it. The top can't be activated again for 1d4 hours. ##
d20Madcap Top Effect
1You spin just like the top. You are [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) for 1 round and then [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
2[_Slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) affects the target.
3[_Shrink_](/Spells.aspx?ID=284) affects you for 1 day.
4A 3rd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) makes you look like the target.
5A statue of the target, made of chocolate or candy, appears adjacent to you.
6Gravity reverses, sending you and the target 30 feet in the air, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). You both fall at the start of your next turn.
7[_Mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) affects the target, and the top loudly recites what it discovers.
8[_Laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) affects the target.
9A fountain erupts from the top, spraying wine for 10 minutes.
10[_Translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) affects you, but you teleport through Hell.
11[_Invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) affects you.
12Four singing skeletons appear to serenade you and your allies for 1 minute, granting a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks.
13The top flings 1 cp at you.
14[_Confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=48) affects the target.
15You and the target change places; this is a teleportation effect.
16[_Banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=19) affects the target, or paralyze if the target's on its home plane.
17Rainbow dye explodes and covers you for 24 hours.
18The top creates a [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) centered on the target.
19A random unattended object within 60 feet of you sprouts animal legs and follows you, reverting when the top's activation recharges.
20Spin again, and the target also spins as though it had activated the top.
name: Madcap Top

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 275

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) contains a seemingly endless supply of bandages, herbs, and healing items of impeccable quality, granting you a +2 item bonus to Medicine checks. If you use the _marvelous medicines_ when you [Treat a Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=56) or [Treat a Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=55), before you roll your check, the medicines attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the poison or disease you're treating, with a counteract rank of 5 and a counteract modifier of +21. This is a healing effect. The medicines can't be used to treat the same affliction for that patient again.

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 138 gp **Bulk** L (when not activated) --- This item appears to be a miniature replica of a theater. It includes a small pocket full of minute set dressing and costumed paper dolls. **Activate—Play with Dolls** 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** You place the miniature theater on the ground, filling it with any set dressing and up to six figures you choose. Then, you tap a rhythm on the miniature, causing it to grow into a modest stage 20 feet wide and 15 feet deep. It's dressed with the decorations you selected, and simple mannequins wear the costumes you chose. A wooden proscenium arch frames the stage, and simple curtains along the sides conceal the wings. As a magical structure, the stage has the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait.
All the stage's set dressing is illusory and disappears if taken more than 20 feet from the stage. The costumes are physical but with illusory embellishments that fade at the same range, revealing only plain, white smocks.
name: Pocket Stage

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Possibility Tome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3029)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An array of semiprecious stones is set into the ornate silver and beaten copper cover of this thick and weighty tome. If you open the book before it's been activated, its vellum pages are blank and pristine, but once activated, words dance and swim onto the pages before your eyes. 

**Activate—Skim** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** As you flip through the book, you think about a broad topic you want to know more about. Choose one skill: Arcana, Crafting, Medicine, Nature, Occultism, Religion, Society, or a single subcategory of Lore. The book's pages fill with information about that skill, though only you can see the information. While the pages are full, you can spend an Interact action perusing the book just before attempting a check to Recall Knowledge with the chosen skill. This grants you a +3 item bonus to the check, and if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead. The information within the book disappears after 24 hours or when the tome is activated again.  

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Possibility Tome

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

---

A grinning skull tops the bow of this macabre key. This key can be used in place of a thieves' toolkit when attempting to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70), and it grants a +1 item bonus to the Thievery check. If the skeleton key becomes broken due to a critical failure on the check, it works as a normal thieves' toolkit and loses its benefits until repaired. 

**Activate—Loosen Lock** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The key casts [_knock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=168) on the lock you're trying to pick.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Slates of Distant Letters](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3031)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276

**Price** 2,450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This matched pair of slates, roughly one handspan wide and tall, have identical ornate frames. Slates are crafted in pairs, and each works with only the other of its pair. If one slate of a pair is ever broken, the other shatters into non-magical shards. The listed price is for a pair of slates. 

**Activate—Send a Message** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You use a piece of chalk to write up to 25 words on a slate. As you write, the writing also appears on the other slate in its matched pair, no matter how far away it is, as long as it is on the same plane. Wiping one slate clean erases the writing from both slates. Each slate can be activated once per hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Slates of Distant Letters

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Usage** held in 2 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Though it appears to be a cloth bag decorated with panels of richly colored silk or stylish embroidery, a spacious pouch opens into a magical space larger than its outside dimensions. The Bulk held inside the bag doesn't change the Bulk of the _spacious pouch_ itself. The amount of Bulk the bag's extradimensional space can hold depends on its type.
You can Interact with the _spacious pouch_ to stow items in it or remove them just like a mundane sack. Though the bag can hold a great amount of material, an object still needs to be able to fit through the opening of the sack to be stored inside.
If the bag is overloaded or broken, it ruptures and is ruined, causing the items inside to be lost forever. If it's turned inside out, the items inside spill out unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. A living creature placed inside the bag has enough air for 10 minutes before it begins to suffocate, and it can attempt to Escape against a DC of 13. An item inside the bag provides no benefits unless it's retrieved first. An item in the bag can't be detected by magic that detects only things on the same plane. Spacious Pouch (Type I) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 75 gp --- 25 Bulk Spacious Pouch (Type II) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 300 gp --- 50 Bulk Spacious Pouch (Type III) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 1,200 gp --- 100 Bulk Spacious Pouch (Type IV) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 2,400 gp --- 150 Bulk
name: Spacious Pouch

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Usage** held in 2 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Though it appears to be a cloth bag decorated with panels of richly colored silk or stylish embroidery, a spacious pouch opens into a magical space larger than its outside dimensions. The Bulk held inside the bag doesn't change the Bulk of the _spacious pouch_ itself. The amount of Bulk the bag's extradimensional space can hold depends on its type.
You can Interact with the _spacious pouch_ to stow items in it or remove them just like a mundane sack. Though the bag can hold a great amount of material, an object still needs to be able to fit through the opening of the sack to be stored inside.
If the bag is overloaded or broken, it ruptures and is ruined, causing the items inside to be lost forever. If it's turned inside out, the items inside spill out unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. A living creature placed inside the bag has enough air for 10 minutes before it begins to suffocate, and it can attempt to Escape against a DC of 13. An item inside the bag provides no benefits unless it's retrieved first. An item in the bag can't be detected by magic that detects only things on the same plane. Spacious Pouch (Type I) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 75 gp --- 25 Bulk Spacious Pouch (Type II) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 300 gp --- 50 Bulk Spacious Pouch (Type III) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 1,200 gp --- 100 Bulk Spacious Pouch (Type IV) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276 **Price** 2,400 gp --- 150 Bulk
name: Spacious Pouch (Type II)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thurible of Revelation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3033)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 277

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This brass censer dangles on a length of chain. Most _thuribles of revelation_ are adorned with swirling [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) text, though some are iron and feature [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117) or [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114) text. 

**Activate—Burn Incense** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))**Cost** incense worth
at least 5 gp;    **Effect** You light the incense inside the censer, and it burns for 1 hour. During that time, as long you are holding the thurible, you gain a +1 item bonus to Religion checks, and any critical failure you roll when you [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22) of a religious nature is a failure instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thurible of Revelation (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 277, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 575

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thurible of Revelation (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 277, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 575

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The thurible grants a +2 bonus. Once per day, when you activate the thurible, you can increase its revelations. During that activation, you can hold the thurible up to your eyes with an Interact action to gain the effects of [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) for 1 round by peering through the smoke. 

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thurible of Revelation (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 277, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 575

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The thurible grants a +3 bonus. The greater version shares the once-per-day enhanced revelations of the moderate version, except peering through the smoke also grants you the effects of [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727).
name: Thurible of Revelation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Traveler's Any-Tool](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3034)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 277

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Before it's activated, this item appears to be an ash rod capped with steel on either end. 

**Activate—Tap** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You imagine a specific simple tool, and the any-tool transforms into it. (Usually, you can choose from a tool listed in the gear from Player Core). This transforms the wooden portion into any haft and the metal caps into spades, hammer heads, or the like, allowing for most basic tools but nothing more complex. You can return the item to its rod form with an Interact action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Traveler's Any-Tool

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 278 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This staff is topped with carved animal and monster heads. While wielding the staff, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Nature checks to identify animals. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Animal Staff **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 278, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 593 **Price** 90 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_know the way_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582)
  • **1st** [_runic body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657), [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694)
Animal Staff (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 278, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 593 **Price** 460 gp ---
  • **2nd** [_animal messenger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441), [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680), summon animal
  • **3rd** [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440), summon animal
Animal Staff (Major) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 278, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 593 **Price** 1,900 gp ---
  • **4th** summon animal
  • **5th** animal form, [_moon frenzy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1609), summon animal
name: Animal Staff

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 279 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This staff of magically hardened wood is topped with a silver sculpture depicting magical runic symbols. A staff of arcane might is a _+1 striking [staff](/Traits.aspx?ID=151)_. **Activate—Sunder** ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** You destroy the staff, unleashing a blast of arcane power in a 30- foot cone, dealing 2d6 force damage per charge remaining in the staff with a DC 30 basic Reflex save. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Staff of Arcane Might **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 279 **Price** 1,900 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
  • **1st** [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631)
  • **2nd** [_everlight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1518)
  • **3rd** [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622)
  • **4th** [_dispelling globe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [_mystic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611), [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
  • **5th** [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), force barrage, [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586)
Greater Staff of Arcane Might **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 279 **Price** 10,000 gp --- A greater staff of arcane might is a _+2 greater striking staff_. Sunder deals 2d8 force damage per charge and is DC 35.
  • **6th** [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492), mystic armor, [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749)
  • **7th** [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), fireball, lightning bolt

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 280 **Price** 425 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This simple iron staff is capped with a faceted, clear gem. **Activate** Interact; **Effect** The gem at the top of the staff glows as a torch, shedding bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) for 10 minutes. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect**
  • **Cantrip** [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)
  • **2nd** [_everlight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1518)
  • **3rd** [_everlight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1518), [_holy light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557)
**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells.
name: Staff of Illumination

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 280 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This ornate metal staff shines with precious inlays of gold. When you Cast a Spell from the staff, the illusory image of something you desire flashes across its surface. While wielding the staff, you gain a +2 status bonus to checks to disbelieve an illusion. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 281 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder agents have access to the this item_ **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- The stone head piece of this smooth wooden staff is carved to look like a lidless eye. While wielding it, you gain a +1 status bonus to Perception checks made for initiative. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Staff of the Unblinking Eye **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 281 **Price** 230 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
  • **1st** [_sure strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
  • **2nd** [_darkvision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723)
Staff of the Unblinking Eye (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 281 **Price** 900 gp ---
  • **3rd** darkvision, [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602)
  • **4th** [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [_detect scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1487), [_telepathy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719)
Staff of the Unblinking Eye (Major) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 281 **Price** 4,000 gp ---
  • **5th** [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661)
  • **6th** telepathy, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)
name: Staff of the Unblinking Eye

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 281 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This oak branch grows leaves in spring that change color in autumn and shed in winter. While wielding it, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to identify plants and fungi. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Verdant Staff **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 281, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 595 **Price** 225 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
  • **1st** [_runic body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657), [_runic weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658)
  • **2nd** [_entangling flora_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515), [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617), [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618), [_shape wood_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1668)
Verdant Staff (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 281, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 595 **Price** 1,750 gp ---
  • **3rd** [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752), speak with plants
  • **4th** oaken resilience, speak with plants
  • **5th** [_plant form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1637), wall of thorns
name: Verdant Staff
